prompt: I'm new to Bayesian stats and I need to model the effect of a new drug on blood pressure. I have pre/post measurements for 80 patients (treatment group) and 80 controls. I honestly don't know what priors to pick — the blood pressure difference could be anywhere from -20 to +5 mmHg based on similar drugs. Also, my boss wants a report she can present to the medical board. Can you walk me through the whole thing?
assertions: Translates domain knowledge (-20 to +5 mmHg) into concrete prior distribution parameters
Mentions PreliZ or prior predictive checks as a way to validate prior choices
Documents prior justifications in the code or notes
Generates a structured report suitable for a non-technical audience
Uses probability language (credible intervals, posterior probability) not frequentist language (p-values, significant)
Explains concepts in an accessible way for a Bayesian beginner
